steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject of the verb is “Each of these republics.” The pronoun “Each” is singular, making the subject singular regardless of the prepositional phrase.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: In a past-tense passive construction, a singular subject requires a singular auxiliary verb. “Was” is the only singular past-tense form that correctly agrees with “Each” and matches the historical context of the passage. The remaining options use either plural forms or a present perfect construction, violating the subject-verb agreement rule. That matches choice C — C is correct.
